mentor-single-real-demo.description: Golden-path mentor demo: one real worker writes one polished Markdown artifact, then reviewer summarizes it.

tracks.title: Polished demo artifact
tracks.instruction: Create a polished Markdown demo artifact for Hermes Guild. The file must be presentation-ready for a mentor: include a short title, one-paragraph goal, a compact architecture flow, a visible input/output contract, a sample task board, and a concise risk/next-step section. Keep it concrete, attractive, and bounded.

review.title: Mentor demo review
review.instruction: Review mentor-demo.md for clarity, completeness, and demo readiness. Write review.md and final-summary.md. Do not invent missing runtime claims; summarize what the artifact demonstrates.

three-part-local-demo.description: Deterministic three-worker local-file-writer demo with visible parallel worker files.

tracks.title: Data model
tracks.instruction: Define the Markdown task board data model, including task fields, status values, sample records, and acceptance notes.

tracks.title: Render UI
tracks.instruction: Describe the visible Markdown task board layout, card rendering rules, empty-state behavior, and UI mismatch risks.

tracks.title: Interaction notes
tracks.instruction: Document interaction notes, smallest verification checks, expected worker outputs, and remaining risks.

review.title: Join review
review.instruction: Review build-1.md, build-2.md, and build-3.md for integration consistency. Write review.md and final-summary.md.

standard-build.description: Build or modify a small usable app feature.

tracks.title: Define contract
tracks.instruction: Clarify the user request into scope, acceptance criteria, allowed files, and output contract.

tracks.title: Implement
tracks.instruction: Produce the scoped implementation or implementation plan and list exact files changed.

tracks.title: Verify
tracks.instruction: Define and run the smallest useful verification checks, then record evidence and remaining risks.

review.title: Join review
review.instruction: Review the contract, implementation, and verification outputs. Request bounded fixes for mismatches; otherwise write review.md and final-summary.md.

debug-fix.description: Diagnose and fix suspicious, failing, or broken behavior.

tracks.title: Reproduce
tracks.instruction: Reproduce or bound the failure with the smallest command, log, or code path. Capture expected versus actual behavior.

tracks.title: Root cause
tracks.instruction: Trace the likely source file/function and explain the smallest safe fix, including risks.

tracks.title: Patch
tracks.instruction: Apply or specify the bounded patch and record files changed plus verification command.

review.title: Regression review
review.instruction: Check that the patch addresses the reproduced issue without widening scope. Write review.md and final-summary.md.

config-provider.description: Configure provider routing, runtime settings, or smoke paths.

tracks.title: Map config
tracks.instruction: Map exact HermesGuildCore repo files and runtime routes affected. Use concrete paths such as docs/incubation/guild-dashboard.html, scripts/guild-dashboard-server.py, _runtime/flock/worker_team_prototype.py, scripts/run-guild-worker-agent.ps1, config/guild/capability-adapters.json, config/guild/provider-adapters.json, and config/guild/planner-skills.json when relevant. Include the word requirements in the requirements summary. Do not invent src/frontend or src/backend paths. Do not expose secrets.

tracks.title: Apply config
tracks.instruction: Describe the smallest reversible config/runtime change using exact HermesGuildCore repo paths. For Retry provider block, the real route is POST /api/task/retry-blocked and the relevant files are scripts/guild-dashboard-server.py, _runtime/flock/worker_team_prototype.py, docs/incubation/guild-dashboard.html, and scripts/run-guild-worker-agent.ps1. Do not invent flags such as enable_blocked_provider_retry, task_execution_adapter, /api/providers/retry-block, or /dashboard/retry-provider-block. Preserve local smoke fallback.

tracks.title: Smoke route
tracks.instruction: Run or specify only bounded non-demo smoke checks using exact HermesGuildCore commands and routes. For Retry provider block, use POST /api/task/retry-blocked and verify that it reopens a blocked task without waking workers. Mention the actual route/file evidence, record whether the route is real provider, local smoke, or blocked, and do not invent dashboard URLs or API routes.

review.title: Provider route review
review.instruction: Verify the route labels are honest, fallback behavior is bounded, and no secrets are exposed. Write review.md and final-summary.md.

rules: Prefer plan-only preview before waking workers.
Do not split tiny requests just to use every worker.
Each worker task must declare output_file, output_artifact, required_skill, and concrete evidence.
Use join_review for fan-in decisions and bounded fix task proposals.
Keep provider routing as capability ammo, not hardcoded worker identity.
